trajectory_summary: This is a short duplicate-lead intercept, not a setter call in the conventional sense. Gabe identified the system conflict within two minutes, made the correct call to defer to the existing rep, and closed cleanly. The call's shape is constrained by the situation — there was no pitch to build energy on, and no energy was lost through rep error. The one coachable gap is the ~28-second unmanaged silence between 01:20 and 01:48 while Gabe worked the system, which left Daniel waiting without a verbal bridge.
outcome_dispute: The v1 outcome tag is listed as 'unknown.' The correct classification is 'duplicate-lead resolved / no appointment set.' This is not a failed setter call — it is a correct procedural outcome. Tagging it as a missed booking would misrepresent the situation and skew rep metrics.

primary_coaching_focus.topic: Verbal placeholder discipline during system holds
primary_coaching_focus.quote: Matter of fact, I was getting ready to send him a copy of my bill.
primary_coaching_focus.why: After Daniel's last statement at 01:20, there is approximately 28 seconds of silence before Gabe re-establishes contact at 01:48. Gabe did attempt brief bridges earlier in the lookup (01:09, 01:12, 01:15), but let the hold go unmanaged after Daniel's final cooperative statement. On a warm, compliant customer this caused no damage — but the same silence on a skeptical customer would read as disorganization or disengagement. Coach Gabe to insert a brief verbal placeholder every 10-15 seconds during any system hold: 'Still here, just pulling this up.' Low effort, high insurance.

key_moments.quote: Perfect. Well, let me go ahead and get this, opened up on the map here so I can take a look at it. While I'm doing that, what's mainly got you guys interested in solar?
key_moments.stewart_read: Gabe asks the emotional anchor question at the right beat and multitasks it with the map pull — efficient pacing. The question never gets answered because Daniel's response reveals the duplicate, but the setup was correct.

key_moments.quote: Matter of fact, I was getting ready to send him a copy of my bill.
key_moments.stewart_read: Daniel volunteering that he was about to send his bill is a strong engagement signal — he was actively cooperating with Ion's process. This is not a coaching failure by Gabe (the call was already in resolution mode), but it is worth surfacing for the manager: this lead is warm and mid-process. The handoff note to Jake should reflect this.

key_moments.quote: Matter of fact, I was getting ready to send him a copy of my bill.
key_moments.stewart_read: The ~28-second gap between 01:20 and 01:48 — after Daniel's last statement and before Gabe re-establishes contact — is the call's only execution gap. Gabe did attempt partial bridges at 01:09 ('And Jake shots you a call?'), 01:12, and 01:15, but those were before Daniel's final statement. After 01:20, there is no verbal bridge for approximately 28 seconds. For a customer who was cooperative and waiting, this is a manageable but real friction point. The reactive read is that Gabe simply went silent while working the system. The strategic read — that Gabe was deliberately quiet to avoid interrupting his own system search — is plausible but doesn't require 28 seconds of silence to execute. Reactive read holds on the narrower gap.

key_moments.quote: Looks like there was, kind of a duplicate here in our system. And so I'll go ahead and get this clear. And, yeah, just keep working with Jake. He he's got you set up, so I'll go ahead and get this clear.
key_moments.stewart_read: Gabe's resolution language is functionally correct — he names the issue, defers to Jake, and exits cleanly. The phrase 'kind of a duplicate' is slightly hedged ('kind of') which could introduce mild confusion about what happened, but Daniel's 'Okay' response shows it landed without friction. No schema section currently covers duplicate-lead routing protocol — this is a schema gap. The manager should note that Gabe handled this correctly by instinct, but there is no documented Ion procedure for this scenario.
